Device-associated infections represent a significant burden in neonatal intensive care. @tbl-device-associated-incidence-density-rates shows infection rates associated with central venous catheters (CVC), peripheral venous catheters (PVC), invasive ventilation (INV), and non-invasive ventilatory support (NIV). By tracking device-associated infection rates separately, you can assess the safety of device utilization in your department and identify specific areas where targeted interventions may reduce infection risk. Lower rates may indicate successful implementation of device care bundles and infection prevention protocols.

:::{.callout-note}
Device-associated infections are defined by a time-based association according to the
[NeoIPC Core Protocol](https://neoipc.org/wp-content/uploads/2023/08/NeoIPC-Surveillance_Core-Module-Protocol.pdf):
a device must have been in use for a defined period prior to infection.
This represents a temporal rather than a proven causal relationship.
:::
